proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear effect on PFS).
Female patients (sex_female=1) have shorter pfs_months than male patients (sex_female=0).
Smoking status modifies pfs_months, with current smokers having shorter PFS than former or never smokers.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear effect).
Adenocarcinoma histology is associated with longer pfs_months than squamous histology.
Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
Brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: OLS coefficient on age_years is +0.170 months per year (p<1e-300). Older patients have longer PFS in this cohort.
Mean PFS is 3.32 months in females vs 3.52 months in males; female - male = -0.20 months (Welch t-test p=7.7e-25).
Mean PFS: never 3.67, former 3.61, current 2.99 months. ANOVA F=433, p=3.7e-187. Current smokers have substantially shorter PFS.
OLS coefficient on ecog_ps is -1.099 months per ECOG point (p<1e-300). Strong adverse effect of poorer performance status.
Mean PFS adeno - squamous = +0.857 months (p<1e-300).
Mean PFS stage IV - non-stage IV = -1.438 months (p<1e-300).
Mean PFS brain mets - no brain mets = -0.921 months (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with egfr_mutation=1 have longer pfs_months than EGFR wild-type patients.
Patients with kras_g12c=1 have longer pfs_months than KRAS G12C wild-type patients.
Patients with alk_fusion=1 have shorter pfs_months than ALK-negative patients.
Patients with stk11_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than STK11 wild-type patients.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than BRCA2 wild-type patients.
Patients with tmb_high=1 have longer pfs_months than TMB-low patients.
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ - EGFR- mean PFS = +0.189 months (p=6.5e-11). Slightly longer PFS with EGFR mutation.
KRAS G12C+ - KRAS- mean PFS = +0.782 months (p=7.4e-94). Longer PFS in KRAS G12C+ patients (likely driven by responders to targeted therapy).
ALK fusion+ - ALK- mean PFS = -0.810 months (p=5.6e-66). Substantially shorter PFS in ALK+ patients (no targeted ALK inhibitor in cohort).
STK11+ - STK11- mean PFS = +0.009 months (p=0.74). No detectable main effect.
BRCA2+ - BRCA2- mean PFS = -0.119 months (p=0.031). Small but significant negative effect.
TMB high - TMB low mean PFS = -0.190 months (p=1.0e-17). Counterintuitively, TMB-high patients have shorter PFS overall in this cohort.
OLS coefficient on pdl1_tps is +0.067 months per unit TPS (p=0.16). No significant main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

Routine labs (hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) each have measurable main effects on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Beta = +0.441 months per g/dL (p=2.8e-109). Strong positive prognostic.
Beta = -0.00051 months per U/L (p=3.6e-5). Small but significant adverse effect.
Beta = -0.0733 months per percent weight loss (p=7.5e-179). Strong adverse prognostic.
Beta = +0.00031 (p=0.78). No detectable main effect.
Beta = +0.0039 (p=0.42). No detectable main effect.

analyses.code: Univariable OLS for each routine lab
analyses.result_summary: All other routine labs (hemoglobin p=0.75, AlkP p=0.14, AST p=0.70, ALT p=0.20, total bilirubin p=0.41, creatinine p=0.22, BUN p=0.51, sodium p=0.48, potassium p=0.33, calcium p=0.29) show no significant main effect on PFS. Hypothesis refuted: most routine labs are not associated with PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls (overall main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls (overall main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls (overall main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated controls (overall main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS treated - untreated = -0.035 months (p=0.081). No overall benefit; trend toward modest harm.
Mean PFS treated - untreated = +0.322 months (p=5.6e-49). Significant overall benefit, likely driven by KRAS G12C+ subgroup.
Mean PFS treated - untreated = -0.025 months (p=0.26). No overall benefit.
Mean PFS treated - untreated = +0.004 months (p=0.87). No overall benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months increases with higher pdl1_tps (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction).
Within pdl1_tps >= 0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.116 (p=0.22). No significant pembrolizumab × PD-L1 interaction.
In high PD-L1 (>=0.5): pembro - control = -0.070 months (p=0.11). In low PD-L1: -0.025 (p=0.26). No benefit in either stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is greater in tmb_high=1 patients (positive treatment_pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction).
Within tmb_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.018 (p=0.68). No significant interaction.
TMB high stratum: pembro - control = -0.048 months (p=0.20). No benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is reduced in stk11_mutation=1 patients (negative treatment_pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction, with stk11_wt patients benefiting more).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.016 (p=0.77). STK11 mutant subgroup: pembro - control = -0.049 (p=0.34); STK11 wt subgroup: -0.032 (p=0.14). No interaction; pembrolizumab provides no benefit in either stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sotorasib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in kras_g12c=1 patients (large positive treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction).
Within kras_g12c=1, treatment_sotorasib=1 produces substantially longer pfs_months than treatment_sotorasib=0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +2.558 (p<1e-300). Sotorasib's effect on PFS is +2.55 months larger in KRAS G12C+ vs KRAS-.
KRAS G12C+ stratum: soto - control = +2.552 months (n=2246 vs 4122, p=1.7e-202). KRAS- stratum: -0.005 months (p=0.80). Effect entirely in KRAS+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in brca2_mutation=1 patients (positive treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction).
Within brca2_mutation=1, treatment_olaparib=1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_olaparib=0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.037 (p=0.78). No significant interaction.
BRCA2+ stratum: olap - control = +0.010 months (n=417 vs 1102, p=0.93). BRCA2- stratum: -0.026 (p=0.24). No benefit even in BRCA2+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Osimertinib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated in egfr_mutation=1 patients (positive treatment_osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction).
Within egfr_mutation=1, treatment_osimertinib=1 produces longer pfs_months than treatment_osimertinib=0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.020 (p=0.76). No significant interaction.
EGFR+ stratum: osi - control = -0.014 months (n=1973 vs 4544, p=0.81). EGFR- stratum: +0.006 (p=0.81). Hypothesis refuted: no osimertinib benefit even in EGFR+ patients in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS model of pfs_months on demographics, comorbidities, mutations, and labs (no treatments) explains a substantial fraction of PFS variance, with ECOG, stage IV, weight loss, albumin, smoking, and histology emerging as the largest standardized contributors.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS of pfs_months on all features except treatments
analyses.result_summary: R² = 0.889 on n=50000. Largest |coef| features: stage_iv (-1.43), ECOG (-1.10), histology squamous (-0.86), brain_mets (-0.92), smoking current/former, albumin (+0.44). Hypothesis supported: clinical features explain ~89% of PFS variance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding the four treatments and their pre-specified biomarker interactions to the multivariable model substantially increases R² beyond the clinical-only model, with sotorasib × kras_g12c being the dominant treatment effect.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS adding treatments and key interactions
analyses.result_summary: R² = 0.927 (vs 0.889 clinical-only). The dominant treatment term is sotorasib × kras_g12c (β=+2.535, p<1e-300). All other treatment main effects and interactions (pembro×pdl1_tps p=0.94, pembro×tmb_high p=0.95, pembro×stk11 p=0.88, olap×brca2 p=0.33, osi×egfr p=0.21, osi×brain_mets p=0.055) are not significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all binary/categorical modifiers (sex, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, EGFR, KRAS G12C, ALK, STK11, BRCA2, TMB high, PD-L1 high, smoking status, histology), at least one treatment_pembrolizumab × modifier interaction reaches p<0.05 with a clinically meaningful magnitude.

analyses.code: Heterogeneity scan: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * modifier') for each modifier
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-value is treatment_pembrolizumab × stage_iv (β=+0.108, p=0.0068); all other interactions p>0.05. The stage_iv interaction implies pembrolizumab is slightly worse in stage_iv=0 patients (β_main = -0.10, p=0.002 in that stratum) and neutral in stage_iv=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond kras_g12c, sotorasib's effect on pfs_months is modified by sex_female, smoking_status, and histology, with the strongest interaction being sotorasib × sex_female (males benefit more than females).

analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib × sex_female: β=-0.562, p=5.3e-41 (largest non-KRAS interaction). Sotorasib × smoking never: β=-0.50, p=3.1e-14; × former: β=-0.21, p=6.9e-6. Sotorasib × histology squamous: β=+0.171, p=1.9e-4. Sotorasib × ALK fusion: β=-0.49, p=4.1e-7. Sotorasib × BRCA2: β=-0.33, p=0.006. Sotorasib × EGFR: β=-0.24, p=1e-4. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists at least one binary/categorical modifier under which treatment_olaparib has a significant heterogeneous effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All olaparib × modifier interactions are non-significant (smallest p = 0.073 for olap × stk11_mutation, β=+0.110). Hypothesis refuted: no robust heterogeneity for olaparib.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists at least one binary/categorical modifier under which treatment_osimertinib has a significant heterogeneous effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Two nominal interactions reach p<0.05: osi × alk_fusion (β=+0.238, p=0.017) and osi × brca2_mutation (β=-0.254, p=0.050). Both involve very small subgroups and the marginal effects in the relevant strata (EGFR+, EGFR-) are null. No clinically meaningful osimertinib heterogeneity detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab improves pfs_months in the compound subgroup pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0 (the canonical biomarker-favorable IO subgroup).

analyses.result_summary: Compound subgroup: pembro - control = +0.056 months (n=1373 vs 1341, p=0.52). 3-way model within PD-L1 high gives pembro main β=-0.156 (p=0.005), pembro:tmb_high β=+0.212 (p=0.04), pembro:stk11_mut β=+0.167 (p=0.24), pembro:tmb:stk11 β=+0.025 (p=0.93). Subgroup-level mean is non-significantly positive but tiny; refined hypothesis only weakly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within egfr_mutation=1 patients, osimertinib produces larger pfs_months gains in patients with has_brain_mets=1 than has_brain_mets=0 (CNS-active TKI rationale; positive osi × egfr × brain_mets interaction).

analyses.result_summary: EGFR+/brain+: osi - control = +0.051 (p=0.64). EGFR+/brain-: -0.029 (p=0.66). EGFR-/brain+: +0.022 (p=0.62). EGFR-/brain-: -0.003 (p=0.91). 3-way interaction β=+0.056, p=0.70. Hypothesis refuted: no osimertinib benefit in any (EGFR, brain mets) cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within kras_g12c=1, sotorasib's pfs_months benefit is suppressed by stk11_mutation=1 (negative sotorasib × kras × stk11 three-way interaction).

analyses.result_summary: KRAS+/STK11 wt: soto - control = +2.587 months (n=1959 vs 3513, p=3.2e-179). KRAS+/STK11 mut: +2.326 months (n=287 vs 609, p=3.6e-25). 3-way interaction β=-0.271, p=0.13. Hypothesis weakly refuted: STK11 mutation only slightly attenuates the effect; sotorasib remains highly active in STK11-mutant KRAS G12C+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation=1, olaparib's effect on pfs_months differs by histology (treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation × histology three-way interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation*histology').fit() and stratified t-tests in BRCA2+
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+/female: olap - control = +0.065 (p=0.72). BRCA2+/male: -0.041 (p=0.81). BRCA2+/ECOG0: -0.137 (p=0.52). BRCA2+/adeno: +0.053 (p=0.71). All BRCA2+ subgroup effects are null. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each treatment's effect on pfs_months is modified by ecog_ps, with better ECOG (0) patients deriving the largest absolute treatment effect.

analyses.code: Stratified treatment effects by ecog_ps in {0,1,2}
analyses.result_summary: Pembro: ECOG0 +0.031 (p=0.35), ECOG1 -0.066 (p=0.013), ECOG2 -0.010 (p=0.82) — small adverse effect at ECOG1. Sotorasib (overall, includes KRAS interaction): ECOG0 +0.31 (p=6e-18), ECOG1 +0.31 (p=7e-27), ECOG2 +0.30 (p=2e-9) — uniform across ECOG. Olaparib and osimertinib: null at every ECOG level. Hypothesis only weakly supported (sotorasib effect roughly equal across ECOG; ECOG does not modify the magnitude markedly).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab's effect on pfs_months is greater (more positive) in current/former smokers than never-smokers (a known IO-response correlate).

analyses.result_summary: Never: -0.008 (p=0.87). Former: -0.047 (p=0.077). Current: -0.029 (p=0.43). All effects are tiny and not significantly different from zero or each other. Hypothesis refuted: no smoking-related heterogeneity in pembrolizumab effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab's effect on pfs_months differs by sex (sex_female × treatment_pembrolizumab interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Males (sex_female=0): pembro - control = -0.044 (p=0.12). Females: -0.024 (p=0.40). Both null; sex × pembro interaction p=0.62 (from earlier scan). Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within kras_g12c=1, sotorasib's effect on pfs_months is concentrated in male patients (sex_female=0) and absent in female patients, i.e. sex_female suppresses the sotorasib effect (negative treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female three-way interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests in KRAS+ by sex; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib*kras_g12c*sex_female').fit()
analyses.result_summary: KRAS+/male: soto - control = +4.644 months (n=1237 vs 2271, p<1e-300). KRAS+/female: -0.012 months (n=1009 vs 1851, p=0.88). 3-way interaction soto:kras:sex_female β=-4.688, p<1e-300. Hypothesis strongly supported: the entire population-level sotorasib benefit is carried by KRAS G12C+ males; female sex completely nullifies the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no subgroup definable from the available features in which treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months by a clinically meaningful and statistically significant margin; the largest pre-specified biomarker-favorable subgroup (pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0) shows no benefit.

The complete subgroup in which treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months is kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (male sex), with an effect of approximately +4.6 months; female sex is the unfavorable feature that suppresses the treatment effect within KRAS G12C+ patients.
There is no subgroup definable from the available features (including brca2_mutation=1) in which treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months significantly.
There is no subgroup definable from the available features (including egfr_mutation=1) in which treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months significantly.

analyses.code: Best pembro compound subgroup: PDL1 high & TMB high & STK11 wt
analyses.result_summary: Best identified pembrolizumab subgroup (PDL1 high & TMB high & STK11 wt): mean diff = +0.056 months (n=1373 vs 1341, p=0.52). Final hypothesis supported: no significant pembrolizumab benefit subgroup exists in this cohort.

Final sotorasib subgroup KRAS G12C+ AND male: mean diff = +4.644 months (n=1237 vs 2271, p<1e-300). Compare KRAS+ female: -0.012 (p=0.88). Effect estimate is signed positive on PFS scale, consistent with hypothesis.
Olaparib in BRCA2+: +0.010 (p=0.93); BRCA2+/female +0.065 (p=0.72); BRCA2+/male -0.041 (p=0.81); BRCA2+/ECOG0 -0.137 (p=0.52). No olaparib benefit subgroup identifiable.

analyses.code: ttest within egfr_mutation=1 (and stratified by smoking, histology, brain mets, sex, stage_iv)
analyses.result_summary: Osimertinib in EGFR+: -0.014 (p=0.81); EGFR+/never_smoker -0.009 (p=0.90); EGFR+/adeno -0.045 (p=0.47); EGFR+/no_brain -0.029 (p=0.66); EGFR+/female +0.086 (p=0.30); EGFR+/stage_iv=0 -0.110 (p=0.26). No osimertinib benefit subgroup identifiable.
